#
# File: ticket290.cbs
# Author: Manfred Jeusfeld
# Created: 2011-09-29/M.Jeusfeld (2011-09-29/M.Jeusfeld)
# ------------------------------------------------------
# Check the new P1 pattern of the semantic optimizer. The P1 pattern
# eliminates the domain predicate In(this,D) in the code generated
# for function definitions if there are appropriate equality predicates
# in the body.
# Start: CBshell -f ticket290.cbs
#


startServer -u nonpersistent -t veryhigh



# here, the domain predicate is In(this,Integer), i.e. the result type of fprobe
# all cases have an equality predicate that makes sure that 'this' is bound
# to an integer

tell "
fprobe in Function isA Integer with
  parameter
    p: Proposition
  constraint
    cfib: $ (p in Individual) and not (p in Class) and (this=0) or
            (p in attributee) and  (this=1) or
            (p in InstanceOf) and (this=3) or
            (p in IsA) and (this=3) or
            (p in Individual) and (p in Class) and (this=100+COUNT(p)) 
        $
end
"

# the CBserver should output in tracemode=veryhigh a line like
#  SemanticOptimizer: In(~this,Integer) guaranteed by ... [P1]


ask "fprobe[1/p]" OBJNAMES  LABEL  Now
#  should return 0

ask "fprobe[Proposition!attribute/p]" OBJNAMES  LABEL  Now
#  should return 1

ask "fprobe[Class/p]" OBJNAMES  LABEL  Now
#  should return 211 (depending on COUNT(Class)
